reasoning: Nvidia had previously been neck-and-neck with Apple, but recent events have widened the gap considerably. Most notably, Nvidia dropped around $600 billion in market cap on January 27, and subsequently experienced a post-earnings sell-off on February 26 following their Q4 report. This dropped Nvidia's market cap to slightly below $3 trillion (before recovering to just above $3 trillion on February 28). In contrast, Apple has maintained a valuation of around $3.67T - $3.9T in early 2025, with expectations of it possibly reaching $4T soon. The gap between Apple and Nvidia is currently roughly $600B+, meaning Nvidia would have to outperform Apple by about 20% in just three months. Given that Nvidia already reported earnings in late February, an immediate massive catalyst is unlikely within the next quarter, making a full recovery to pass Apple by late May quite difficult. This justifies adjusting the crowd's recent 19% estimate substantially downward.
